name: Aquaman
quantumsTake: Aquaman is a champion who was built for crowd control, making him incredibly useful for PvP game modes and certain levels of Training Simulator. He pairs really well with Justice League lineups, particularly ones designed around Superman. He's really more of a Supporter than Firepower, as his damage output is embarassingly low, but he makes up for that with an incapacitate ability in addition to his ultimate, which disrupts enemies by pulling them into a vortex. Overall, he's a solid champion with a niche use, so be careful not to overinvest.

name: Atom
quantumsTake: Atom saw a significant rework in 2025, taking his kit from utterly unusable to moderately effective against certain Training Simulator levels, and to an extent in PvP modes such as 3v3 Strategic Challenge. The stun on his ultimate, coupled with significant AoE damage prior to his departure from the battlefield, can be the push you need to complete levels you otherwise struggle with. He doesn't need much investment (if any), so he's just a good champion to have in your level sync just in case.

name: Arsenal
quantumsTake: Arsenal saw a rework in 2025, and it actually made him if not worse, net neutral to his prior position, which was unusable. He gained slight synergy with Teen Titans, but he offers very little of value. In testing, we found that Green Arrow (a legendary champion) can do just as well as he can in most battles. Do not build..

name: Bane
quantumsTake: Bane is a bit of an enigma in the game. He has incredible self-sustain, but he doesn't see much play. He has some niche uses, such as popping tires on Killer Tank in Combat Cycle. I also think he is worth building for Training Simulator and PvP if you go with a villain arc to your account. We are collecting more data on Bane and want to see him with full force investment at high stars to form more of an opinion.

name: Batgirl
quantumsTake: Batgirl is a supporter who was out of favor in the meta for quite a while, but has become quite useful in [Bat Family] lineups utilizing Nightwing. She does require some investment to get the full potential from her kit. Fair warning: She's a niche champion at the moment, so make sure you're all-in on [Bat Family] and [Teen Titans] before you commit.

name: Batman
quantumsTake: Batman is one of the most iconic DC characters, and a lot of us in the community expected more out of his kit. He was much better in the beta, but he's more mediocre than anything in the current version of the game. He's quite useful in Training Simulator, and I've seen him used to great effect with Nightwing in PvP. Non-spenders who get lucky and obtain a couple copies of him, or lite spenders who are willing to purchase his starter pack can get a good jump on Combat Cycle early on, but there are just too many Bleed champions who can outpace him.

name: Black Adam
quantumsTake: Black Adam is one of the more useful Legendary champions. He's the most obtainable Spiritual DMG champions in the game and the only one you can get as a new player without spending until The Joker shows up in the Bleed. What I like about him is you can build a budget Legion of Doom team using him instead of Sinestro or Deathstroke. As your roster progresses, he's going to taper off, but he's going to be usable in every game mode for the first month or two after you start, and for that reason you can get away with investing in the first level or two of his force.

name: Captain Cold
quantumsTake: Captain Cold has seen a complete 180 in the game, from being considered one of the worst champions to one of the most important for non-spenders looking to improve their Combat Cycle scores. His ultimate functions much the same as Cyborg's, but only for single-target. So there are some clear limitations to the kit, but ever since the developers fixed his ultimate, we've seen him added to some interested Physical DMG lineups and he has finally found his niche spot in the meta.

name: Catwoman
quantumsTake: Unfortunately, Catwoman is the worst champion in the game. In the beta, she was one of the best Combat Cycle champions, due in part to her ability to steal stats from enemies. However, she saw a nerf during the soft launch of the game that has set her back and there are just too many better options in the Warrior class.

name: Constantine
quantumsTake: Constantine is one of the best AoE chmapions in the game, and he's on a team that is relatively obtainable by non-spenders and lite spenders. He has an AoE on both his ultimate and one of his basic skills, and you can get a third AoE if you have his unique legacy piece (which is required if you're building Justice League Dark). He does heavily rely on his teammates (at least a three-tag bonus) to provide additional Spiritual AMP, but he sees plenty of action as a plug-and-play unit when there's a support champion who can boost ATK..

name: Cyborg
quantumsTake: Cyborg is one of the best early game mythic champions simply because he can be used at zero stars, since his ultimate is his primary utility. He does tend to taper off as you enter late game (Captain Cold offers similar utility, albeit single-target, and you get him to higher stars faster), for whales and long-time players, this is a champion that you will use heavily in Combat Cycle and other non-PvP modes like Anti-Crime Network.

name: Deadshot
quantumsTake: Deadshot has a solid kit for single-target damage, but he seems to be lacking something to make him viable. His only faction tag is Suicide Squad, and with the lack of a meta lineup for that faction, he will continue to be one of the worst mythic champions in the game. I do not recommend building him at this time, but we will monitor the meta with upcoming champion releases and update our rankings accordingly.

name: Deathstroke
quantumsTake: Deathstroke is one of the more devisive champions in the game, primarily because he requires heavy investment and isn't very plug-and-play friendly at lower star levels. Yet, he has some of the highest single-target damage of any champion, and Legion of Doom teams continue to make up a large percentage of the top teams in Meta Brawl. You need him at two purple stars for minimum viability, but one you get him there, he's one of the champions that can really carry your account.

quantumsTake: Doctor Fate has been one of the better F2P champions in the game since the beta tests, and that's because his ultimate works really well to protect your team even at 0 stars. He's a bit squishy at lower star levels, but becomes quite formidable as you build him, and he is plug-and-play viabile in all game modes.

name: The Flash
quantumsTake: The Flash is one of the most important supports in the game for anyone looking to maximize their damage in Combat Cycle. He doesn't need to be on a specific team to provide a massive advantage, because his kit is meant to cycle your other squad members' ultimates. In early tier lists I did not value him highly and I regret it. I see him staying relevant for a long time, and cannot wait to see the rest of the Flash Family.

name: Green Arrow
quantumsTake: Green Arrow is one of the best legendary champions and ends up being useful well into the late game, especially for non-spenders. On manual, he can be used to deal with tough to kill enemies in Training Simulator, and he's actually a solid damage dealer in Combat Cycle if you don't have access to any of the high value limited mythics.

name: Green Lantern
quantumsTake: Obviously Hal Jordan is a pay-to-win champion, but he's an incredible healer at high stars. I can't in good conscience recommend that anyone spend $15,000 to $20,000 USD just to unlock him at five red stars, but if you're able to get him to purple stars over time, you'll finally be able to start inserting him into your Justice League lineups. Mera getting her Justice League tag back does mean that Green Lantern is less needed, but he was S-tier before his legacy piece came out, and now he's solidly entrenched in that ranking.

name: Harley Quinn
quantumsTake: Harley Quinn is one of the most important early champions for non-spenders and lite spenders. Since you get a copy of her through the 7-day login reward, it means that any additional shards allow you to build her abilities. She does decent AoE damage, especially with her legacy weapon, which is given to you early on in your playthrough. She does taper off as you get access to limite mythics, but I think she will see a resurgence as soon as there's a renewed focus on Suicide Squad champions.

name: Hawkgirl
quantumsTake: Hawkgirl has been completely reworked as of April 2026, and she has legitimate staying power in the meta as a plug-and-play champion. I think she might prove useful in some Bat Family comps with Orphan. She has incredible spiritual damage resistance thanks to her Nth Metal Aegis effect. They also doubled her damage output, and her Thanagarian Mace is now a must-use for her kit if you're trying to make her work competitively. Still a niche champion at this point, but she will definitely work her way into the meta in the future.

name: Killer Croc
quantumsTake: With the prevalence of Legion of Doom in the top of Meta Brawl, Killer Croc is an interesting champion. I think he's a solid Guardian to build early on, but he tapers off as you get access to other champions. I see him as a bridge to Scarecrow for new accounts, and if you're not going to run Legion of Doom, you can skip building him entirely.

name: Martian Manhunter
quantumsTake: Martian Manhunter is a champion we expected to have more synergy with Justice League, so it was a surpise to see him as the main engine for the Outsiders team. He's a direct counter to Justice League Dark and a natural PvP workhorse. As the game gets more Outsiders champions, he should become even more valuable. Unfortunately, he's a high investment champion, as he needs to be 1 yellow star with a 5 blue star legacy piece minimum. He just saw a rework in April 2026 and he is considerably better agains Spiritual AOE. He's just on the cusp of being an S+ champion. Not a must-build, but he has a ton of plug-and-play synergy.

name: Mera
quantumsTake: Mera is the first healer you get in the game, and she scales well with your account. There are several healers that are much better than her, but given the high acquisition cost of champions like Starfire and Green Lantern, the only healer worth prioritizing over Mera early on is Krypto, in particular if you invested in Superman. The big downside to her kit is there's no utility outside of raw healing, but she heals well enough to somewhat offset that limitation. Despite falling off late game due to power creep, unless you're a heavy spender, chances are you'll still be using her six months into your account.

name: Nightwing
quantumsTake: Nightwing is one of the more free-to-play bleed champions due to the versatility in lineups he can fit into. Obviously Bat Family is quite expensive and a long farm if you can't spend, but if you can get him to purple stars or higher and obtain his legacy weapon, it will vastly improve any roster.

If this attack defeats the target, Nightwing immediately strikes again on another enemy with the lowest HP, dealing DMG equal to [%] of his ATK. Nightwing is untargetable during the leap. Prioritizes the enemy Champion with the lowest HP in auto mode.

name: Poison Ivy
quantumsTake: Poison Ivy underwent a complete kit overhaul in early 2026. She appears to be a much more efficient healer now, but there are questions about whether her Super Armor is viable anymore or even matters. Superman seems to be a little better agains the vine AI, so that's a downside for PvP, but an upside for Justice League mains in Training Sim going against her. Overall, I think she's a great early game healer, even at low stars, and I would 100% recommend using the free copy you get. However, once Riddler enters the Bleed on your server, she will become utterly irrelevant.

name: Raven
quantumsTake: Raven has a kit that can be hard to manage. She's much better on manual, where you can time her ultimate. I've seen very effective PvP comps using her, especially on early servers, but she end up being most effective in a Justice League Dark lineup.

name: Red Hood
quantumsTake: Red Hood is a champion that can be fairly useful when you first start playing, but his kit suffers from one fatal flaw, and that's the fact that when he uses his ultimate, he usually dies soon thereafter. We are hoping that his kit gets refreshed when he ascends to mythic, but until then, he just doesn't slot into any team late game.

name: Red Robin
quantumsTake: Red Robin's kit has been reworked again as of early 2026, and they really made him objectively worse. Unfortunately for Nightwing mains, if you're trying to go for best tag synergy, he's the only guardian champion for Bat Family. So he goes from decent to marginally useful. Definitely better champions worth building out there, but he may be a sacrificial build early on while you try to ramp into a team with more Bleed champions.

name: Robin
quantumsTake: Robin is effectively discount Nightwing. If you're looking to invest in Bat Family long-term for an eventual Nightwing team comp, you'll want to go all-in on Robin while you wait for Nightwing to show up in the Bleed. And in a lot of cases, you can use both of them together for a one-two punch that's hard to beat in all game modes. Note that Robin was reworked in early 2026 and the consensus is he is slightly worse now but that he still functions as an early Nightwing replacement while you build your Bat Family and wait for Nightwing to show up.

The first slash targets a selected enemy (the farthest enemy in auto mode), whil subsequent slashes hit random enemies near the initial target. Robin is untargetable during the attack.

name: Scarecrow
quantumsTake: Scarecrow was the best tank in the game until early 2026, when his kit was gutted due to how overpowered his stun mechanic was at the beginning of fights. They went further, though, and made him very good against physical damage, but a joke against spiritual damage. He's still usable, as he's required for LOD in PvP and he's still decent with Arkham.

name: Shazam
quantumsTake: Shazam was not well-regarded earlier in the beta testing for the game, but that was mostly because many players didn't have him at high star levels. Now that the game has matured, Shazam is heavily regarded as the best early tank for a Superman hypercarry team for fresh accounts. He's more of a tool for lite spenders and above, because you need all of his skills unlocked at a minimum for usability, but don't sleep on Shazam's taunt and revive if you need a big frontline for Training Simulator and even early PvP before you have access to better champions.

name: Sinestro
quantumsTake: Many people assume that Deathstroke is the primary win-condition for Legion of Doom PvP teams. However, Sinestro is who really shuts down enemy teams. His ultimate is one of the best burst damage abilities in the game, and it's even better with his unique legacy Sinestro Corps Ring. He's an S-tier champion because not only is he the star of LoD, but he can also be added to JLD teams to bolster Constantine's ability to deal Spiritual damage.

name: Stargirl
quantumsTake: Stargirl is a niche champion who at one point was regarded as a potential game changer in PvP. Unfortunately, her cleanse is only useful against The Drowned in Combat Cycle, and even then she needs to be at high star levels for her kit to be effective. Do not invest in her force, but know that she does have a very specific purpose. This is a champion that we will monitor to see whether future champions being added to the game changes her usefulness.

name: Superboy (Conner Kent)
quantumsTake: Superboy is a champion that we always knew would be a great support from the time we saw his kit, but who we did not realize would be as good as he is as a plug-and-play champion. You can stick him into just about any lineup and see massive gains, which can't be said for champions like Zatanna, who is good but only with her assigned tag synergy. Superboy does work best with Supergirl, but I think he's a champion that all players should pull for, even if it's just to white stars, granted that Purple 1 will future proof him as your account progresses if you're a lite spender and can afford to pull that high.

name: Supergirl
quantumsTake: Supergirl is widely regarded at this point as one of the best AoE champions in the game, if not the best. However, she also requires some of the highest investment in the game. You NEED Krypto and Superboy paired with her at a minimum to get the full effectiveness from her kit, so she gets the same treatment at LoD champions in that I can't in good faith recommend that non-spenders go for Superman Family unless they are willing to hamper their account by pulling for her and waiting for her re-run to get her up and running. Build her at your own risk if you don't spend, but know that once she's at yellow stars, she is unstoppable.

name: Superman (Clark Kent)
quantumsTake: Superman is up there with Batman as one of the most iconic DC characters. He's the first Bleed champion you can go after on a fresh server. As far as his kit goes, he's one of the most powerful single-target damage dealers in the game. When fully built, and with the right supporters, he can carry your roster in every game mode. The biggest limitation is that long-term you will need his unique legacy item and purple stars or better to realize his full potential.

name: The Joker
quantumsTake: The Joker has seen quite a few changes to his kit in the last few months, but he's maintained a relatively stable spot in the meta. He may be the best champion in the game for Combat Cycle, particularly on bosses where there are multiple targets, and he is incredibly useful in Training Simulator for the same reason. However, he is less than ideal for use in Meta Brawl and 3v3, due to the fact that there are so many champions who can counter his kit by shutting him down early. It's hard to give him the S-tier designation due to his PvP deficiencies, but he's a champion that you can 100% build around and he is especially useful for new accounts.

name: The Penguin
quantumsTake: The Penguin received a kit rework in early 2026. We still see him as a skip for anyone who builds The Joker up long term, but for non-spenders who need a third or fourth team and have no other choice, the new kit actually has some viability in the early game and for those looking for a solid ship combat team.

name: Two Face
quantumsTake: Two Face has seen his ups and downs in our rankings since the game's launch. His kit bolsters Spirutal damage dealers, and he does Physical damage, so it's a bit counterintuitive. Effectively, he's much more of a supporter for a Joker hypercarry than a true damage dealer, but that synergy with The Joker is what makes him useful. he doesn't see play in PvP, but he's especially useful on the Combat Cycle bosses with multiple targets and Training Simulator.

name: Vixen
quantumsTake: Vixen is a champion with some very strong synergies for new players on fresh servers, but she falls off incredibly fast due to the fact that Birds of Prey is not a meta faction and there are much better Mythic and Limited Mythic supports you can unlock in your first month, such as Cyborg. I wouldn't invest too much force into her, but she does have some early usefulness.

name: Wonder Woman
quantumsTake: Wonder Woman is one of the first champions you're given as you start the game's main campaign, and she maintains her usefulness in your roster well into the late game. With the new Amazons tagged champions being added into the game, she now fits with Justice League, Justice League Dark, or Amazons, making her one of the most verstile champions available. Her stuns can easily win PvP fights, and her tankiness lends her to effective use in Training Simulator as well. Her biggest weakness is Combat Cycle. Note: Once Wonder Woman ascends to Mythic, her shards can currently only be obtained from the Meta Brawl store.

name: Krypto
quantumsTake: Krypto is easily one of the best healers in the game, and one of the most impactful champions released so far. He has a unique kit in that he does no damage. Instead, he provides massive amounts of burst healing, healing over time, and shields. In addition, he is very important as the primary engine for generating Krypton Energy for the Superman Family team. In my opinion, he's a must build champion for everyone from non-spenders to whales. His biggest limitation is that in PvP he waits several seconds to use his ultimate after it's ready to be used. This results in a lot of dropped battles and creates more of a coin flip situation in mirror matches and against opponents that need to be shut down early. But, if you're able to cast his ultimate, he can knock down enemies for a short duration which effectivley acts as a stun. Even given the limitation with his ultimate timing, he should still be used in PvP, and he's incredible in Combat Cycle as well, particularly since he can pop addiitonal tires on Killer Tank and stun summoned enemies on Merciless.

Each bone he finds grants him a large amount of Energy and increases his healing effect by 5%, stacking up to 50%.

name: Ra's al Ghul
quantumsTake: Ra's al Ghul has a very unique kit, allowing him to sacrifice his HP in order to deal more damage. He's a champion that needs to be protected by shields in order to work properly. I've showcased an LOA team with him, Talia, Fate, Hippolyta, and Deathstroke, where he can drop down to 1 HP and have a constant shield on him, which allows him to shut down LoD. He's incredible in PvP because of how his kit works, but tapers off in other aspects of the game. I think he will be a much more well-rounded champion as we get more LoA champions.

name: Starfire
quantumsTake: Starfire is the first Limited Mythic healer added to the game, and accordingly has pretty powerful stat scaling. She does perform best when used with Titans, particularly Nightwing, but she can be inserted into a lot of lineups and be quite efficient. She's good in both PvP and Combat Cycle and has solidified herself as an S-tier champion. You can get away with taking her to purple stars for most players. Her legacy piece is a must buy, even before you have her unlocked. Note that she received a slight rework in early 2026, but so far that rework has resulted in either net neutral or net positive effects. In other words, early advice still stands. She's the best healer in the game.

name: Talia al Ghul
quantumsTake: Talia operates similarly to Ra's with her ability to sacrifice HP to deal damage, and she has the ability to heal herself when enemies are defeated. I would still characterize her as more of a niche champion because her ultimate is too conditional. She does work with Ra's in high-level PvP, but she's more beneficial in Combat Cycle and other PvE modes where champions remain above 50% HP for longer. Personally, I do not think she is worth the investment for non-spenders and lite spenders at this time.

name: Zatanna
quantumsTake: Zatanna is one of the best supporter champions in the game, especially since she can actually deal a little bit of damage on top of being there to super charge your Constantine. You will hear a lot of people say that she doesn't require a lot of investment, and while it's generally true that she performs well enough at low blue stars or even 5 white stars, getting her to purple stars or better will really ramp up her kit. In my book, she's a certified Whale, though you can get away without buying her legacy piece if you're a non-spender or lite spender.

name: Queen Hippolyta
quantumsTake: Queen Hippolyta is the first new Amazons tagged champion to come to the game, and her kit is built around defensive support through granting shields to her teammates and absorbing damage taken by them. Her ultimate is a channeled ability, but it grants her Super Armor, meaning she cannot be interrupted during the cast. We expect her to be used primarily with Amazons once the rest of that team is formed, but she is also one of the more plug-and-play champions we've seen in quite a while. Some have dubbed her Doctor Fate 2.0 and I think that's an apt comparison. I think all rosters should try to incorporate her into their farming plans because she is quite good even at blue stars without her unique legacy piece, but if you want to use her in high-level Meta Brawl or 3v3, you'll need to get her to Yellow 5 with a White 5 unique legacy piece, or Red 5, if you want her to instantly use her ultimate at the start of battle.

name: Swamp Thing (Alec Holland)
quantumsTake: Swamp Thing's kit makes it seem like he should be a damage-dealing warrior, but it turns out that his usefulness is more as a support to keep Constantine alive, who is your true win-condition for JLD. His ultimate and first basic skill will both reduce the enemy's current Energy, which will reduce the amount of times they can attack in battle. On top of that, Swamp Thing's [Swamp] ability will create an area of effect damage reduction zone, and he will summon it to start battle. His unique legacy piece Antler Crown is a must-use for him, too, because in addition to providing passive healing over time for the duration of battle, it will further limit your opponent by reducing their ATK and Crit Rate.

name: Wonder Woman Death Metal (Diana Prince)
quantumsTake: Death Metal Wonder Woman is one of the most powerful AND most fun champions in the game. Unfortunately, she was behind a massive paywall at the time of her release, and you cannot obtain her in any way unless they do a re-run of her special event. However, if you were fortunate enough to obtain her at purple stars or better, she can be plugged into any team in any game mode for a huge boost. Her kit heavily relies on high Physical DEF and it ramps the more [Amazons] champions you use. Her legacy piece is a must buy for her and provides a large amount of Crit Rate and additional damage output, as well as a large shield.

name: Wonder Girl (Donna Troy)
quantumsTake: Wonder Girl was initially panned as a required unit for Amazons, and many wanted that to be the case. Unfortunately, she has turned out to be a very mediocre champion. You could certainly run her in a full Amazons team to try and add a little extra DPS for your DMWW, but she just doesn't really have a spot in the meta right now, and is not worth investing in unless you're a whale who was going to max her out otherwise. She's capable of some chip damage, but her true strength is in making her allies more powerful.

name: Nurse Harley (Harley Quinn)
quantumsTake: Nurse Harley has proven herself to be a formidable champion in the Arkham's Most Wanted lineup. The issue is she costs more than any champion in the game to max out, as she is the current VIP track reward, and her legacy piece (which is also paywalled) is 100% required for her to perform as intended. I really like her kit and the damage over time mechanics, and she's a visually fun champion with the syringe gun. We can only hope she becomes more affordable over time.

name: The Riddler
quantumsTake: The Riddler certainly shook up the meta when he appeared in the bleed for early servers. Once he shows up, your server will likely already be in a pretty substantial shield meta. Any supporter that has a shield is going to be favored over raw healing, and with Conundrum Cane The Riddler can actually do both! What we love about Riddler's kit is that he can also debuff your enemy. While early in a fight the debuffs are randomized, if you can last through a few of his ultimates, you'll actually be able to max out the stacks of ALL the debuffs he lands. He's an auto include in many top meta PvP lineups.

name: Artemis
quantumsTake: Artemis is a unique champion with a Duel (taunt) mechanic, which makes her a very compelling high-risk-high-reward champion for PvP combat. If she wins her initial duel, it will catapult your team into a victory if you have another high damage win-condition champion, like DMWW. Her unique legacy weapon is a must if you build her, making her an expensive investment.

name: Lobo
quantumsTake: Lobo is a bit of a wildcard for DCDL. We just don't have a good idea where he's going to fit into the meta. He's got the Suicide Squad tag, which has historically been a bad/incomplete team in the game. What we do know is that he can dish out some serious damage. He's just limited by the fact that you have to pull apart better teams to build around him. So he's great with Combat Cycle, but I don't advise you pull for him until we see some more lifter champions for his tag synergy. If you do go for him, make sure you've got the cash to take him to yellow stars or higher, and you MUST buy his legacy weapon.

name: Death Metal Batman
quantumsTake: Death Metal Batman is one of the most powerful champions in the game. As with Death Metal Wonder Woman, though, he was locked behind a massive paywall on release and we expect him to be difficult to obtain on a re-run as well. He has a very unique kit, in that he can't be targeted directly as long as one blackest bat and one ally are still alive. The blackest bat summoning mechanic is incredible, and when most of your team dead, the damage really ramps up.

name: The Flash (Wally West)
quantumsTake: Wally West was a highly anticipated champion on release, but he falls flat at all but the highest investment level. He needs high end transmutes to even remotely be useful. We thought he would shut down teams with heavy shield usage (think Hippolyta + Starfire + Riddler comps) and deal massive damage himself, and while he does have some utility to slow down shield teams, his Ultimate takes so long to ramp to the highest damage level that matches usually end before he does much work.

name: Orphan (Cassandra Cain)
quantumsTake: An interesting take on Cassandra Cain for sure. The Birds of Prey synergy makes me think they wanted her to work with hybrid teams that combine with Bat Family synergy, so I think she works really well with comps that include Nightwing, Hawkgirl, and Batgirl, with a fifth that ramps damage (Zatanna or Superboy) or protects the team (Riddler, Hippolyta, or Starfire). She's not the hypercarry we were expecting, but she certainly makes Bat Family a much more interesting tag synergy. Her legacy piece, as we are noticing with newer champions, is required and needs heavy investment.
